title_en: Three ways the LNG market could crack before winter
title_zh: 液化天然气市场在冬季到来前可能“断裂”的三种情况
rubric_en: War, weather and outages may still send gas prices soaring
rubric_zh: 战争、天气与停运仍可能让天然气价格再度飙升

paragraphs.en: ON JUNE 28TH the Hlaitan, a tanker laden with liquefied natural gas, set off for France from Freeport, Texas. Almost immediately it changed course. TotalEnergies, its charterer, had received a better offer from Asia. According to Kpler, a data firm, the Hlaitan is due at its new destination on August 1st.
paragraphs.zh: 6月28日，一艘装载液化天然气的油轮“海莱坦号”从德克萨斯州弗里波特起航前往法国。几乎立即，它就改了航线。租船方道达尔能源（TotalEnergies）收到了来自亚洲的更高报价。据数据公司Kpler称，“海莱坦号”预计于8月1日抵达新的目的地。

paragraphs.en: Such stories have become common. Buyers in Asia, where a scorching summer is turbocharging demand, are outbidding Europeans for American cargoes at every turn. LNG tankers are being diverted in record numbers (see chart 1). In sidelined Europe, gas storage, drained by a cold winter, is only 47% full—the lowest in 15 years at this point of the summer (see chart 2).
paragraphs.zh: 这类故事如今越来越常见。亚洲买家在持续高温的夏季里对美国产货源的需求被迅速推高，在每一轮竞争中都在压过欧洲买家。液化天然气运输船正以创纪录的数量被调去亚洲（见图1）。在欧盟一侧，经过严寒冬季后，欧洲天然气储存量已被抽得只剩47%，这是夏季这一时点15年来最低水平（见图2）。

paragraphs.en: Behind this is a lack of supply from Qatar, usually Asia’s main provider. Since America and Iran agreed in mid-June to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, 11 LNG tankers have traversed the waterway—better than none, but just three days’ normal traffic. On July 7th America began striking Iran, in retaliation for Iranian attacks on tankers (including a Qatari LNG one a day earlier). President Donald Trump also revoked a waiver which allowed Iran to sell its oil and declared the ceasefire with the Islamic Republic to be “over”.
paragraphs.zh: 其背后是卡塔尔供应不足——卡塔尔通常是亚洲的主要供应方。自美国和伊朗于6月中旬同意重开霍尔木兹海峡以来，已有11艘液化天然气船队经过该海峡——比此前的“零船次”要好，但仅相当于正常运力的三天。7月7日，美国开始对伊朗发动报复性打击，以回应伊朗对油轮的攻击（包括一天前对一艘卡塔尔液化天然气船的袭击）。美国总统唐纳德·特朗普还取消了允许伊朗出售石油的豁免令，并宣称与伊朗的停火“已结束”。

paragraphs.en: Unlike oil prices, which despite an uptick amid the latest hostilities are well below their war-time highs, gas prices remain 55% higher in Europe, and 73% higher in Asia, than in February (see chart 3). Analysts expect Qatari flows to be back to normal by September, just in time for Europe to restock before winter. But a more malign scenario is still uncomfortably plausible.
paragraphs.zh: 与油价不同的是，油价虽然在最近一轮冲突中有所回升，但仍远低于战时高点；天然气价格仍比2月高出55%（欧洲）和73%（亚洲）（见图3）。分析人士预计卡塔尔的供应到9月将恢复正常，正好让欧洲有时间在入冬前补库。但一个更不利的情景目前仍然并非完全不可能。

paragraphs.en: The war in Iran has deprived the world of a fifth of its LNG flows. It has also knocked out 17% of Qatar’s export capacity, which will take years to rebuild. Restarting surviving plants takes time. Despite extra supplies from Africa and Australia, lured by rising prices, and lower demand owing to Asian countries’ power-saving measures, industrial users’ deferred purchases and the restarting of coal-fired plants, the world will be short of a cumulative 40m tonnes of Qatari LNG this year. That is almost a tenth of last year’s supply.
paragraphs.zh: 伊朗的战争已经使世界失去了五分之一的液化天然气（LNG）运输量。它还使卡塔尔的出口能力下降了17%，而这需要数年时间才能恢复。幸存电厂的重启也需要时间。尽管非洲和澳大利亚受到高油价吸引，增加了供应，且亚洲国家的节电措施降低了需求、工业用户推迟了采购，以及煤电厂陆续复产，但今年世界仍将缺少累计4000万吨卡塔尔LNG。这几乎相当于去年供应量的十分之一。

paragraphs.en: And that is before Asian and European importers start replenishing their winter stocks. Most are waiting as long as they can. One reason is that prices remain high; another is that prices for winter deliveries are no higher than today’s. If Qatari supplies return by September, Asian demand for American cargoes should ease, spot prices fall and European buyers restock. By November EU stores would be some three-quarters full—low, but enough for a normal winter. Prices ought to stay under control.
paragraphs.zh: 而这还没有算上亚洲和欧洲进口商开始补充冬季库存的时候。多数进口商都尽量等到最后一刻。原因之一是价格仍然很高；另一个原因是冬季交付的价格并不高于当前价格。如果卡塔尔供应在9月恢复，亚洲对美国运载货船的需求应会放缓，现货价格将下跌，而欧洲买家也会开始补库存。到11月，欧洲的储量约有四分之三满仓，虽然偏低，但足以支撑一个正常冬季。届时价格应当会保持在可控范围内。

paragraphs.en: But the market is so tight that a succession of small shocks could precipitate a crunch. Three risks stand out. First, Gulf supplies may not return on time. Hours after the Qatari gas tanker was hit near the entrance of the strait on July 6th, another heading for Hormuz turned around. A dozen ballast tankers are waiting near Ras Laffan to load. But repeated skirmishes, let alone a collapse of the ceasefire, could rattle buyers and push Asia to bid even harder for American cargoes.
paragraphs.zh: 但市场目前已经非常紧绷，一连串小冲击就可能引发供应挤兑。主要有三大风险。第一，海湾地区的供应可能无法按时恢复。7月6日，卡塔尔一艘输气油轮在海峡入口附近遭到袭击，数小时后，另一艘前往霍尔木兹海峡的油轮就掉头返航。现在已有十几艘空载油轮在拉斯拉凡附近等待装载。但一再发生的摩擦，甚至更有可能出现停火破裂，都可能动摇买家情绪，并促使亚洲市场对美国货源出价更高。

paragraphs.en: A second risk is extreme weather. In June Europe suffered record temperatures, which pushed up demand for cooling and so power (see chart 4). In Asia El Niño—a climatic phenomenon that brings hot, dry weather to the region—is also pushing the mercury abnormally high, forcing countries such as Bangladesh and Pakistan back onto the spot market. More sizzling weather in either continent will mean more buying.
paragraphs.zh: 第二个风险是极端天气。6月欧洲出现了创纪录的高温，推高了制冷需求并进而抬高了用电需求（见图4）。在亚洲，厄尔尼诺——这一会带来高温和干旱的气候现象——也正在把气温推得异常偏高，迫使孟加拉国和巴基斯坦等国家再次转向现货市场。无论在欧洲还是亚洲出现更多炽热天气，都将导致更多采购。

paragraphs.en: China’s unpredictable purchases add uncertainty. The world’s largest LNG importer mostly buys under long-term contracts. It does not stockpile gas as it does oil. What it does not need it resells, as it did in March and April. But when heatwaves struck in May and June, it resumed large imports; when welcome rain fell in late June, it retreated. Scorching temperatures might again cause it to buy in a hurry.
paragraphs.zh: 中国不可预测的采购行为增加了不确定性。这个全球最大的液化天然气进口国主要通过长期合同购气，做法与石油不同，它不会囤积天然气。它多余的采购会转手抛售，就像三月和四月所做的那样。但当五月和六月发生热浪时，它又恢复了大规模进口；而六月底凉雨及时到来后，它又开始退潮。炎热的气温再次袭来时，可能会促使其匆忙采购。

paragraphs.en: The third danger is that infrastructure fails. Prolonged dry spells drain hydropower reservoirs. Power prices in Switzerland have already jumped as it imports electricity from neighbours that use fossil fuels, notes Gillian Boccara of Kpler. If rivers are abnormally warm, nuclear reactors, which need cold water, may throttle back.
paragraphs.zh: 第三个风险是基础设施故障。持续的干旱会耗尽水电库容。正如克利帕尔公司的吉莲·博卡拉（Gillian Boccara）指出，瑞士的电价已经上升，因为它正在从使用化石燃料的邻国进口电力。如果河流水温异常偏高，需要冷却水的核反应堆可能会被迫降载。

paragraphs.en: Then there are unknown unknowns. A big LNG terminal could break down, as when the Freeport plant, then accounting for 4% of global supply, caught fire in 2022. Strikes, like those in Australia in 2023, can disable facilities for weeks. Russia—which supplied record volumes to Europe this spring—could turn off the taps.
paragraphs.zh: 还有那些“未知中的未知”。一个大型液化天然气终端可能出现故障，就像2022年占全球供应4%的弗里波特工厂起火那样。像2023年澳大利亚那样的罢工，也会让设施停摆数周。俄罗斯——今年春季向欧洲输送了创纪录的天然气量——也可能会“掐住”水龙头。

paragraphs.en: Several of these shocks at once could turn the manageable into a nightmare. “When bad news accumulates,” says Anne-Sophie Corbeau of Columbia University, “the effect on prices can turn exponential.” There is little scope left for switching fuels. South Asian countries will surely have to cut back. China, Japan and South Korea are rich enough to keep bidding. Europe will not freeze this winter. But it could face an enormous heating bill.■
paragraphs.zh: 多重冲击叠加，可能让本来可控的局面变成一场噩梦。“当利空消息不断累积时，”哥伦比亚大学的安妮-索菲·科博（Anne-Sophie Corbeau）说，“对价格的影响会呈指数级上升。” 转向其他燃料的空间几乎已经不大了。南亚国家肯定不得不削减需求。中国、日本和韩国的支付能力足够，仍能持续竞价。欧洲这个冬天不会出现停电或供暖全面停摆的情况，但可能要承担一笔巨额采暖账单。■

paragraphs.en: For more expert analysis of the biggest stories in economics, finance and markets, sign up to Money Talks, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter.
paragraphs.zh: 如需更多关于经济、金融与市场重大事件的专家解读，请订阅《Money Talks》——我们的每周订阅制通讯。
